theme.description: A premium, structural aesthetic relying heavily on extreme contrasts and minimalist geometry, avoiding the common 'tech' look of purple gradients and soft shadows.

headings.guidelines: Never center headings by default. Use explicit alignment and hierarchy. H1 max text-5xl or text-6xl. This provides a strong, geometric, and distinct look.

body.guidelines: Left-align dense content. Perfect for data-heavy dashboard tables and clarity, creating a slight 'editorial' tension against the geometric headings.

mono.guidelines: Use for raw data, QR code IDs, technical specs, and stats.

colors.guidelines: Color is Function Only. 95% of the UI should be monochrome (blacks, whites, grays). Use the Signal Red (#FF3B30) strictly to guide action or show active states. Never use default blue or generic purple gradients.

surfaces.header: Crystal Glass: bg-white/70 or bg-black/60 with backdrop-blur-xl and border-b. MUST not be purely transparent over content.
surfaces.cards: Grid Borders concept. Use border-collapse style. Subtle 1px borders, NO soft shadows. Flat background. Hover states should feature slight lift and a sharper border, not a blurry shadow.
surfaces.textures: Kill digital flatness with subtle noise/grain overlays on hero backgrounds and large marketing surfaces.

marketing.grid: The 'Tetris' Grid. Asymmetrical grid structures. col-span-full md:col-span-8 row-span-2 for the Hero visual.
marketing.spacing: Generous Spacing (py-24 to py-32). Containers with borders must have internal padding (p-6 or p-8).
marketing.components: Use pure HTML/Tailwind for marketing layout structures, not Shadcn structural components, to allow maximum creative freedom.

dashboard.structure: Collapsible left-sidebar (240-280px) + Main Content Area. Move away from top-bar navigation.
dashboard.density: High Density Grid. gap-4 or gap-6. Surface 5-7 core KPIs first (Progressive Disclosure).
dashboard.components: Use Shadcn components extensively but heavily customize them. Strip out default soft shadows. Use sharp 1px borders with a max 4px border-radius.

feature_specific_guidelines.qr_wizard: For the Super Admin QR creation wizard, use Progressive Disclosure. Do not show all 30+ types at once. Group them into distinct categories (e.g., URL, Media, Business) with icons. Use a live preview pane on the right side (sticky) while the form scrolls on the left.
feature_specific_guidelines.analytics_dashboard: Use Actionable-First Layout. Realtime scan analytics (world map, device breakdown) should use Recharts. The world map/charts should use the monochrome palette with Signal Red (#FF3B30) for data highlights.

motion.marketing: Framer Motion for scroll-triggered asymmetrical reveals. Staggered children animations for lists/grids.
motion.dashboard: Minimalist. Hover lifts (-translate-y-1), subtle shadows, fast state transitions. No disruptive animations. Use sonner for toasts.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text meets APCA standards against backgrounds. ALWAYS explicitly set text color on hover (e.g., hover:bg-slate-900 hover:text-white).
accessibility.focus: Focus states must have HIGHER contrast. Use focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#FF3B30] focus:outline-none.

images.description: Abstract gray building - use as a structural, premium background for marketing hero or login split-screen.
Black android smartphone on white textile - use for showcasing the QR code scanning flow on marketing page.
Corporate team meeting - use for social proof or 'built for teams' section.

media.icons: Use lucide-react. Standardize stroke-width to 1.5 for a premium feel.

components.charts: Use Recharts. Keep them clean, avoid default colors. Use primary (#FF3B30) and monochrome shades.
components.forms: Use Shadcn forms, inputs, selects. Avoid native <input> or <select>.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Always add transitions for specific interactive elements excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container (i.e., do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file).
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
NEVER use placeholder image URLs (via.placeholder.com etc). Use ONLY the provided image URLs.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
